2606.16595.title: ArtNet: A JEPA-Like Articulatory Predictive Framework for Robust Zero-Shot Phoneme Recognition
2606.16595.paper_summary: Zero-shot cross-lingual phoneme recognition is often hindered by the fragility of direct acoustic-to-symbol mapping, which is susceptible to language-specific variations. Echoing joint-embedding predictive architecture (JEPA) work in vision, we propose ArtNet, a framework that explores a structured feature prediction task based on articulatory features to enhance acoustic robustness. Specifically, ArtNet integrates an articulatory predictor, designed to extract universal articulatory representations from self-supervised learning (SSL) features, with a variational information bottleneck (VIB) to suppress language-specific variations. Experiments on seven unseen languages demonstrate that ArtNet, particularly when synergized with the proposed vector-space inventory alignment (VSIA) strategy, significantly outperforms competitive baselines, achieving a 20.56\% relative reduction in phoneme error rate (PER) and 7.01\% in phoneme feature error rate (PFER).
2606.16595.paper_summary_zh: 零樣本跨語言音素識別常常受到直接聲學到符號映射脆弱性的阻礙，這種映射容易受到語言特定變異的影響。呼應視覺領域的聯合嵌入預測架構（JEPA）工作，我們提出了ArtNet，一個基於發音特徵探索結構化特徵預測任務的框架，以增強聲學穩健性。具體而言，ArtNet整合了一個發音預測器，旨在從自我監督學習（SSL）特徵中提取通用的發音表示，並與變分信息瓶頸（VIB）結合，以抑制語言特定變異。對七種未見語言的實驗表明，ArtNet，特別是在與提出的向量空間庫存對齊（VSIA）策略協同作用時，顯著超越了競爭基準，實現了20.56\%的音素錯誤率（PER）相對減少和7.01\%的音素特徵錯誤率（PFER）減少。
2606.16595.author: Zeqian Hu et.al.
2606.16595.authors: Zeqian Hu, Fuliang Weng, Shu Shang, Yaqian Zhou
